message:
feat(acp): migrate file I/O to async asupersync, add size limit, and modernize idioms

Significant improvements to the ACP (Agent Client Protocol) module:

File I/O migration to async:
- resources/read: std::fs::read_to_string → asupersync::fs::read with
  String::from_utf8_lossy for binary-safe reading
- resources/read: add 10MB file size limit check via asupersync::fs::metadata
  before reading, returning a clear error with byte counts on oversize files
- resources/write: std::fs::write → asupersync::fs::write (async)

Idiomatic Rust improvements:
- Introduce AcpSessionsMap type alias for
  Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<AcpSessionState>>>>> — used in run()
  and validate_file_path() signatures to reduce visual noise
- map_or(false, ...) → is_ok_and(...) for boolean lock-guard checks
  (prompts/cancel abort check, session exists checks)
- if-let-else blocks → map_or_else for session list construction
- match Ok/Err → if-let-Ok-else in validate_file_path path resolution
- Inline format variables: format!("...{}", var) → format!("...{var}")
- .map(PathBuf::from).unwrap_or_else(...) → .map_or_else(..., PathBuf::from)
  for cwd resolution in handle_session_new

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
